fields.name: Salvation
fields.description: Capital of the Holy Republic of Luxen and the most beautiful city in the world: white marble and gold leaf behind walls that have never fallen, the Shepherd's crook on every wall, and beneath it all, catacombs older than the Republic. Salvation counts its people in castes — the Blessed, the Humble, the Honest, the Simple, and the Sinners — and it is holy, and it is certain, and nobody Gifted stays. Whether you leave by the Citadel's sanctioned gate or by a door in the dark no one living remembers, every story that begins in Salvation is the story of leaving it.

fields.name: Umbros
fields.description: Capital of the Umbral Empire and the realm the others call evil — a Britannia-scale power of severe blackstone holds and baroque Alarician estates, whose motto is Tradition and Steel and whose one true faith is strength. It claims a meritocracy and mostly isn't; it prizes the Gifted, the ambitious, and the dangerous more honestly than any pious neighbor. Umbros wears its darkness openly and is amused to be called wicked for it — but it broke the Tyrant of Chains once, and it will put to death anyone who enslaves a will by magic. Every story that begins here leaves through the Black Spire, charged to return puissant.

fields.name: Inferna
fields.description: Capital of the Grand Principality and seat of the Grand Princess: an island city of grand Reefian palazzos, open galleries drinking the sea wind, and harbors full of ships that fly their reputation like a flag. The continent calls the Principality a den of pirates and witches, and calls its people Imps; the Isles put the insult on their banners and praise Envala for the sea and reefs. Beneath the laughter sits a long memory — Cinderus, taken by Luxen and never answered for — and a defiance that has outlasted every fleet sent to teach the Isles manners. Every story that begins in Inferna crosses the sea to the ghost city, and walks the Echoes to Shroudwatch.

fields.name: Ariwn
fields.description: Capital of the Kingdom of Ariwn, where the sun has not risen in living mortal memory: the Blood Patriarch's veil holds eternal night over gothic castles that count few windows and impale intruders on their spires. The vampiric Peerage rules forever — succession is a formality no house has needed in centuries — with Dhampir heirs waiting out eternity, human households sworn for generations, and the Lycan war clans bound to the crown by ancient blood pacts. Nothing in Ariwn changes quickly, which is exactly why the Gifted matter: every story that begins here is a new piece on a very old board, and the whole Kingdom is watching it move.

fields.name: Aythirmok
fields.description: The Northlands, where winter is the kind season. Aythirmok survives on inverted instincts: frost beasts prowl the snows, but it is the spring thaw the Aythir dread, when the legions of the Deadlands march on the softening fields. South of Gloria's Scar the holds are built to be dismantled and carried north in a night; salt and steel wait at every hearth, and trust is not given to anyone who has not wintered for it. A Glimpse here is a hold's hope against the marching dead. Every story that begins in Aythirmok walks south with everything the hearth could spare, toward the ghost city and the watchers' school beyond it.

fields.name: Luxen Port
fields.description: A bustling port city in the Luxen realm.
